config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society is strongest when individual rights are protected first, because once governments or majorities can override them too easily, abuse becomes very hard to stop. In my view, the interests of society matter a lot, but they should be pursued through fair rules that respect personal freedom, not by treating the individual as secondary. From my perspective, a community is healthier when people feel protected, not sacrificed.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat terlalu mudah bagi “kebaikan bersama” dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menekan minoritas atau membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; justru itu pagar yang membuat masyarakat tetap adil dan tidak berubah menjadi tirani mayoritas. Tentu masyarakat punya kepentingan nyata, seperti keamanan publik dan kesehatan bersama, tetapi itu harus dicapai dengan batas yang jelas dan alasan yang sangat kuat. Kalau hak pribadi dianggap sekunder, saya rasa kita sedang membuka pintu bagi kebijakan yang tampak efektif di permukaan tetapi merusak kebebasan dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual, because in practice that often becomes a license for the state or the majority to overreach. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand why people value social harmony and collective responsibility, but I also know how easily “for the common good” can be used to silence dissent or ignore minority rights. Society does matter, especially on issues like public health or disaster response, yet those cases still need clear limits and accountability. If individual rights are treated as secondary, the result is not a stronger society but a more fragile one, because people lose trust that their dignity will be protected.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena menurut saya hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, bukan dikalahkan oleh kepentingan masyarakat begitu saja. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya sangat curiga ketika “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran terhadap minoritas, pembungkaman kritik, atau perluasan kuasa negara tanpa kontrol yang ketat. Memang benar masyarakat punya kebutuhan nyata, seperti keamanan dan kesehatan publik, tetapi tujuan itu harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil dan proporsional, bukan dengan mengorbankan kebebasan dasar. Kalau hak individu dibuat sekunder, yang sering terjadi bukan masyarakat yang lebih kuat, melainkan masyarakat yang lebih mudah menyalahgunakan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “the public interest” can be used to pressure individuals, minorities, or critics when limits are weak. Social harmony matters to me, but harmony that comes from silencing people is not real stability; it is just controlled quiet. Public health, safety, and disaster response are legitimate reasons to restrict some freedoms, but those restrictions must be narrow, accountable, and temporary. If society always comes first, then the individual becomes too easy to sacrifice, and that is a dangerous path.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena menurut saya hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, bukan dikalahkan oleh kepentingan masyarakat begitu saja. Dari pengalaman dan nilai yang umum di Amerika, saya curiga ketika “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penekanan minoritas, pembungkaman kritik, atau perluasan kuasa negara tanpa pengawasan yang ketat. Memang masyarakat punya kebutuhan nyata seperti keamanan publik dan kesehatan bersama, tetapi tujuan itu harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau hak individu dianggap sekunder, yang sering muncul bukan masyarakat yang lebih kuat, melainkan tirani mayoritas yang lebih mudah menyalahgunakan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
